title: EEG Preprocessing
description: Raw EEG is bandpass filtered (0.5–45 Hz), artifacts removed via independent component analysis, and segmented into 2-second epochs for stable spectral estimates.
footnote: Artifact rejection removes eye blinks, muscle, and channel drift before source localization.

title: Source Localization — eLORETA
description: eLORETA inverts the forward model to estimate where cortical activity originates, mapping 64+ scalp electrodes to ~10,000 cortical vertices, then parcellating into 68 Desikan-Killiany regions.
legend: J = cortical current density · T = inverse operator · V = scalp voltage
footnote: Spatial resolution ≈ 5–7 mm with 64-channel montage; ~1 cm with low-density arrays.

title: Connectivity Analysis — wPLI
description: Weighted Phase Lag Index measures functional connectivity between all 2,278 region pairs across 5 canonical bands (δ, θ, α, β, γ). wPLI is robust to volume-conduction artifacts.
legend: Sxy = cross-spectrum between regions x and y
footnote: Unlike raw coherence, wPLI rejects zero-lag (volume-conducted) components.

title: Deficit Matrix
description: Comparing stroke connectivity against a healthy baseline reveals which pathways are broken and by how much. False-discovery-rate correction controls for the massive test count.
legend: i, j = region indices · b = frequency band
footnote: FDR-corrected across 11,390 tests (2,278 region pairs × 5 bands).

title: DTI Tract Routing
description: Streamlines from the HCP tractography atlas that pass through the lesion mask are flagged as destroyed. Their intra-cavity segments are extracted and smoothed with cubic B-splines.
snippet: destroyed = target(streamlines, affine, lesion_mask) → extract_segment() → splprep(k=3)
footnote: Curved channels follow real anatomy rather than naive straight lines.

title: Channel Prioritization
description: Each channel's diameter scales with the functional deficit magnitude of the connection it restores. Stronger deficit → wider channel → higher axonal carrying capacity. Placement uses priority ordering with collision detection.
legend: Range 100–300 µm · Max 30% cavity fill · Minimum wall 50 µm
footnote: Priority-first packing guarantees the most critical connections are placed before geometry saturates.

title: Scaffold Assembly
description: Curved channel tubes are boolean-subtracted from the solid cavity mesh. Porous infill is added between channels for vascular ingrowth. The final scaffold is exported as .STL for bioprinting.
footnote: Mesh validated for watertightness, manifold surfaces, and no self-intersections before export.
